episodes.title: You Want More Intimacy, But How Do You Get It?
episodes.description:
Mismatched intimacy needs can feel personal, painful, and deeply confusing especially in queer relationships shaped by shame, silence, or religious messaging around desire. If you’ve ever wondered whether wanting more closeness, sex, or emotional connection means you’re “needy” or “asking for too much,” this deep dive is for you.

In this Thrive session, we explore what intimacy actually means, why desire ebbs and flows, and how to talk about it without turning your relationship into a battleground.

✨ In this deep dive, we explore:
• Emotional vs. physical intimacy and why both matter
• How religious trauma impacts desire, consent, and communication
• The difference between pressure and invitation in relationships
• How to express intimacy needs without shame or ultimatums
• What to do when compromise feels hard or impossible

episodes.title: Heated Rivalry, Gay Men, Straight Women, and Traitors | A Therapist's Reaction
episodes.description:
Heated Rivalry has been out for months and as a therapist for queer couples I hear about it all the time. 
And obviously I have watched it, because any time there are members of the LGBTQIA+ community centered in media, I am checking it out. 

I don't know about your algorithms, but my feed is FULL of people's "hot takes" on straight women, gay men, and the fandom of heated rivalry. In addition, there is the very recent situation that happened on the Peacock show, Traitors, between Michael Rapaport and Colton Underwood, regarding sexuality, being closeted, and how and when you come out. 

Believe it or not, these are all connected. Check out this week's video to see how, to understand some of the perspectives behind some of the viewpoints, and to see how you may be able to respond if this discourse hits closer to home. #heatedrivalry #traitors #lgbt 


In this week's episode of Ask a Therapist, I am reacting to Heated Rivalry and the discourse around it.

episodes.title: Therapists, the Queer Community, and the Religiously Traumatized | The StoryTellers
episodes.description:
I recently heard someone say that there was a weight in being a storyteller. A pressure and a responsibility to get things right, and to be fair and accurate. 

I also think there is a power in storytelling. You can help people see themselves and their stories or the narratives they have been given in new light. Or you can help people see options for a positive, lighter future. 

As a therapist and as a youtuber I feel like I have carried the role of storyteller for people, but I also feel that anyone in the LGBTQIA+ community or anyone who has left a religion and tried to heal ALSO bears that burden. 

So today's video is about that, the lessons I have learned this year, and a bit of a 2025 wrap up. Maybe this video goes nowhere, maybe it means something to just a small number of you, I don't know. But it was an important one for me to make, so here it is. #storytime #religion #lgbt

episodes.title: The Newest Problem with Mel Robbins | A Therapist Reacts to NYT OP ED
episodes.description:
Well, it is time. I am finally telling you what I actually think of Mel Robbins and her pseudo intellectual "advice", especially in light of her recent op ed in the New York Times. 

She co authored this piece with Dr Karl Pillemer, a professor and researcher at Cornell University. It, along with her podcast with the same person, covers Dr Pillemer's research of 80, 90, and 100 year olds over the last 20 years and the messages we should take from it. Which is not in general a negative message, except that they then take that to mean we should all stop "holding grudges" and "forgive our families" so we "don't regret it". 

And if you have been around me for any length of time, you probably know already why that was a problem for me. So check out this week's edition of Ask a Therapist to hear how this whole thing breaks down, and what the actual truth is so that YOU can be ready when faced with these same attitudes. #melrobbins #family #askatherapist

episodes.title: When Gratitude and Mindfulness Become Trauma
episodes.description:
If gratitude or mindfulness ever made you feel worse instead of better, you’re not imagining it. Religious trauma can completely distort these practices — turning them from tools for peace into tools of control.

In this video, I break down how gratitude and mindfulness are often weaponized in high-control or toxic religious environments, how that distortion impacts trauma survivors, and how to begin reclaiming these practices in ways that feel safe, authentic, and healing.

You’ll learn:
🌿 What forced positivity and performance-based gratitude look like
💔 Why “everything happens for a reason” isn’t mindfulness — it’s spiritual bypassing
💡 How to rebuild healthy gratitude and mindful awareness without religious guilt
💬 Practical, trauma-informed steps for reconnecting with yourself and your body

If you grew up in a religious environment that taught you to ignore your pain, silence your doubts, or feel ashamed of your body, this episode is for you. Healing is possible — and it starts with separating what’s real from what was taught to control you. #religioustrauma #meditation #gratitude

episodes.title: What Is Christian Nationalism and Why Should You Care?
episodes.description:
Things are...odd right now, I think we can agree. Everything feels off, and sometimes it is hard to tell why. 

Recently, we can trace it back to the tone set by those in power in most of our countries. In the USA, Christian Nationalism is a bigger presence than ever. It impacts policy, relationships with neighbors, family relationships, and how people are able to deal with their jobs on the regular. 

This week's episode of Ask a Therapist Will define Christian Nationalism, help you understand where it came from and how it became the massive force it is today, and how you can respond to it in your own life. 

If this video helps you in any way, and you feel comfortable doing so, please share it. I don't envision this topic going very far algorithm wise without a little assistance from people's shares and likes, esp right now. #christiannationalism #patriot #askatherapist

episodes.title: I Kissed Purity Culture Goodbye
episodes.description:
Did you grow up with any of these messages?
- That's too much skin for her to be wearing to school/work/in a professional setting
- Boys will be boys
- Saving yourself for marriage will make sex AMAZING
- Dating is practice marriage
- Courting is better than dating
- Guard a girl's heart 

These and many others are the messages of purity culture. In 1997, Joshua Harris published a book called I Kissed Dating Goodbye, which became the vehicle for much of the mainstreaming of purity culture into teen spaces, at least in a way that was more overtly talked about. 

This week's episode of Ask A Therapist is all about Purity Culture, how to disentangle yourself from it, how *I* disentangled MYself from it, how centering men plays into our ideas of ourselves, and what to do about, well, any of that. 

Drop a comment below sharing your experience with purity culture, I kissed dating goodbye, or anything like it! #purityculture #exvangelical #askatherapist

episodes.title: Religious Trauma in the LGBTQIA+ Community: How to Move On
episodes.description:
If you're a member of the LGBTQIA+ community and grew up in a religious environment, chances are you've felt the impact of religious trauma.  The shame, fear, or silence that came from simply being who you are is not unique to our community, but it is intensely present. 

In this week's episode of Ask a Therapist, we’ll explore what religious trauma is, how it uniquely impacts queer folks, and most importantly, how to begin healing and moving forward. Whether you’ve left religion entirely or are still processing the messages you were taught, this space is for you.

🔹 Topics Covered:
Naming religious trauma in queer lives
Common internalized beliefs from religious settings
How to begin healing (even if you’re not “over it”)
Practical steps for reclaiming joy, autonomy, and identity

Feel free to share your story in the comments...you're not alone, and you sharing may just help someone else know that too. .

episodes.title: What is Religious Trauma? | Ask a Therapist
episodes.description:
Were you taught that being queer was sinful? That your body was shameful? That questioning meant rebellion?

If you were raised in a high-demand religious environment, especially as a queer person, chances are, you’ve experienced **religious trauma**.

In this video, I break down what religious trauma really is (spoiler: it's not just "church hurt", but I talk about the difference between the two, or lack thereof), where it comes from, how it shows up in your life, and why it hits so deeply for those in the LGBTQIA+ community.

You’ll learn:
🔸 What religious trauma actually looks like  
🔸 How it affects your brain, body, and relationships  
🔸 Why “just leaving” religion doesn’t always stop the pain  
🔸 Where to begin your healing journey  

This is for the overthinkers, the boundary-strugglers, the ones still afraid they might be “too much” or “not enough.”

Drop a comment below and tell me: What’s one religious message you’re working to unlearn?

Don’t forget to like, subscribe, and share if this helped you. 💜

episodes.title: My Religious Past Gave Me Anxiety in My Present | A Therapist Explains
episodes.description:
Are people who grow up in churches more likely to have problems with anxiety? 
Are they more likely to struggle with anticipating people's needs and worrying about how they are perceived?

The short answer, yes.

People who grow up in religion carry much of that messaging into adulthood, and with it the expectations and understanding of self. Because of that, and because they grow up thinking everything they think, do, and say is watched and judged by a being on high, there is a HUGE amount of anxiety that comes  along with just making daily choices.

Check out this week's Ask A Therapist episode to find out more reasons why people who grow up in high demand or any religion are more likely to have anxiety issues, and how to deal with it in your adulthood once you find out. 
 #anxiety  #deconstruction #exvangelical

episodes.title: How Purity Culture Warped Our View of Women| A Therapist Explains
episodes.description:
"You should smile more"
"You can catch more flies with honey than you can with vinegar"
"That wasn't very nice"
"Pretty girls don't have ugly mouths"

Have you every heard or said any of these? Society has very specific rules and expectations for women and how they should navigate interaction in the world. 

And if a woman violates them, it is her looks, her kindness, and her intelligence that gets questioned or insulted. 

Have you ever wondered why that is?

Purity Culture. That's why. 

In this week's edition of Ask a Therapist we will discuss why this has come to be, and how we can stop perpetuating the expectation that women owe the world softness, sweetness, and pleasantry.  #purityculture #women #askatherapist

episodes.title: The Emotional Manipulation of the Christmas Story | A Therapist Explains
episodes.description:
Ah the Christmas Story. Cinematically it has everything. Young love, a mean ruler, a poor couple that we are meant to root for, and of course some angels and some danger and some intrigue. 
When you dig deeper, though, that story is used over and over again to make people feel obligated to support the Church as a whole...after all, what type of person are you if you don't appreciate an adorable, blameless baby coming to earth in a beautiful and unassuming way? Aren't you thankful for that gift? 

*insert eye roll*

For many years the story of Christmas has been a feel good guilt trip, one you don't even know you are going on. So check out this week's Ask a Therapist video for a breakdown of how it is done so you can spot it for yourself, and decide what you want to do about it. #christmas #exvangelical #deconstruction

episodes.title: What You Need to Understand About Religious OCD
episodes.description:
Religious OCD. There's a lot being said about it and frankly, much of it is not true. I find it pretty common to mash parts of it together and ignore other areas of importance. Because there is so much misinformation out there, I thought I would take an episode of Ask a Therapist and attempt to do a base explanation of what Religious OCD is, what it is not, and what to do if you think you may have it. 

It is important to remember that you don't have to have Religious OCD if you have some of these symptoms, and having religious trauma doesn't automatically mean you will have religious OCD. This is a very serious diagnosis and if you think you may have this, you should see a Mental Health Professional. Watching this video is not sufficient to diagnose for Religious OCD or any other disorder. episodes.title: What is a Microaggression and Why Should I Care | A Therapist Explains
episodes.description:
I know by now you are all too aware of how the world discriminates against the LGBTQIA+ Community. But are you aware of the subtle ways that bias and discrimination manifest against us as well? In this week's episode of Ask a Therapist, we dive into the world of microaggressions, shedding light on five common yet harmful behaviors that impact LGBTQIA+ individuals daily. Join us as we unpack the following microaggressions:

1. Assumption of Heteronormativity and Cisgender Identity: Discover how assuming everyone is heterosexual or cisgender can marginalize those who identify differently.
2. Invalidating Someone's Identity: Learn about the effects of dismissing or undermining someone's gender identity or sexual orientation.
3. Heterosexist or Cisgender Jokes: Understand why seemingly harmless jokes rooted in heterosexism or cisgenderism are damaging.
4. Exclusionary Language: Find out how language that excludes or ignores LGBTQIA+ identities contributes to a hostile environment.
5. Questioning the Validity of Their Identity: See the impact of questioning or challenging the authenticity of someone’s LGBTQIA+ identity.

Through understanding these issues, I'm hoping to help us all become aware of these everyday microaggressions. Whether you're part of the LGBTQIA+ community or an ally, this video aims to provide a perspective on fostering inclusivity and respect. Don't miss out on this crucial conversation—hit play and let's work together to create a more accepting world!  #lgbt #educationalvideo #askatherapist

Ever feel like growing up in church messed with your self-esteem, like, a LOT?  (Spoiler alert: You're not alone!)

Self esteem can be hard to come by these days, but especially if you grew up in churches and had to deal with any of the messages below (and then some). In this week's Ask a Therapist Video, we are going to get into how growing up in religion could have negatively impacted your self esteem, because it has come to my attention lately just how many adults are walking around out there still carrying the baggage foisted on them from hearing some very, VERY not ok things very early on in their brain development. 

In this video, we're getting real about how church life can sometimes lead to those impossible expectations to be "perfect." Thinking your problems don't matter, because church first, your feelings... later? Maybe never?
Sometimes it meant bottling it all up. Doubts? Mental health struggles? Let's just pretend they don't exist! The toxicity of the creation story, especially for women, can be a real kick in the teeth.

If this sounds familiar, grab a cuppa (or your beverage of choice) and let's chat! We'll explore how to ditch the guilt, build healthy self-esteem, and move forward in life without all that baggage.  #religiousbeliefs #selfesteem #askatherapist

episodes.title: How Purity Culture Impacts Coming Out Later in Life
episodes.description:
Purity Culture has impacted so many things in our lives.

And I have another one for you...it has impacted when we come out, too. 

Due to the things that Purity Culture has told us about our worth, our focus and our function in life, people (especially women) are so much more likely to ignore their own needs and, honestly, desires. 

When that is happening, and when the message you get is so clearly "everyone else first, and that will satisfy you", is it surprising that it takes so long to know our true selves?

This week's Ask a Therapist video is all about how Purity Culture has impacted our lives, and caused people to come out much later than they would have if that had not been a message handed to them so strongly. Check out our video and let us know what else you want us to cover (or recover!) about purity culture and its impacts.

And I do not usually answer, for a lot of reasons. Not the least of which is because this channel has become a place where people can feel safe to tell their stories, and it is not ok with me to have that invaded by giving airtime to make people feel better about their own faith. 

But a lot of you asked, and so here we are. 

I don't think this video is going to make me any friends. It will probably lose me a few. But also, It needs to be said. So here it is.   #askatherapist #lgbt #affirming

episodes.title: Young Life Kicked me out for being Gay, Now what do I do?
episodes.description:
As a Therapist, I am often asked my opinion on things. I rarely give it, at least in session.  That is part of the reason I started this channel, to give people access to a therapist's honest responses. 

Well, as honest as possible. 

Today we are going old school Ask a Therapist with an actual letter that was sent to me by a viewer. 

She is a college student who was volunteering with Young Life, and when she went to a leader to talk about her confusion around her sexuality, she was removed from leadership, lost her housing, and lost her friends and extended social circle. 

This week's Ask a Therapist video will deal with some of the toxic messages coming out of churches and para churches toward the queer community. We will outline a few of them specifically, and explain why they are untrue and unfair. Be sure to stay until the end, for some practical tips on what to do when you lose your religious community by someone else's actions and a message all of you should have heard months or years ago. 

I have full permission to read and respond in this video.

episodes.title: Crazy Things I Believed as a Christian | Ask a Therapist
episodes.description:

Recently someone asked me if I still considered myself a Christian. And I was floored by the emotions that ran through me.

episodes.title: Shame in Adulthood | A Therapist's Perspective
episodes.description:
Recently I've been putting a lot of thought into unifying experiences. And one unifying experience I keep coming back to is when we were children, having our needs NOT met resulted in us all feeling shame about ourselves in our adult years. This shows itself differently in everyone, but the root need we are trying to address remains the same. 

Shame, guilt, fear, punishment, reward...
Are those words linked for you? Or are they different? How did you experience them in your childhood, and how do you experience them now? In this week's episode of Ask a Therapist we will investigate the roots of childhood shame, and how it impacts us in our adult lives. 
#shame #askatherapist #adulthood

episodes.title: Is Christianity an MLM? Ask a Therapist
episodes.description:
Do you know what an MLM is? A multi Level Marketing Structure is a popular business model for companies that appeal to (mostly) women. They sell their employees on a life that makes them free and offers all sorts of adventures...if they sell to their friends and family, get THEM to sell for them also and oh, yes, invest their own money too.

So in this week's Ask a Therapist video, we will discuss whether or not Christianity is an MLM, and how it did or did not come to be that way! #mlm #religioustrauma #askatherapist

episodes.title: Religion and Authority Issues; a Therapist Explains
episodes.description:
Do you ever get nervous when people tell you they need to talk to you?
Do annual job reviews derail your day, even if they are good?
Do you ever wonder if you do enough for your friends?

Issues with people in Authority are common in people dealing with religious trauma, deconstruction, and reclaiming their own processes. That's due to the fact that being in a religion, especially an authoritative one, creates in you a need to please authority in order to find value in your existence. You're taught to do that. But it doesn't have to stay that way. 

In this week's Ask a Therapist video, we will deal with those authority issues (even the ones that don't come from Religious Trauma) and explain where they came from and why.  
#religioustrauma #askatherapist #authority

episodes.title: "Are you SURE you are Gay?" A Therapist's Response
episodes.description:
This week we go to a subscriber's question about how to respond to someone in their life. This writer told their Stepdad that they are gay and Step dad's response was "Are you sure? How do you know? "

 I think you probably know where this is going. It is hard enough to have to pan how and when you tell the truth of who you are to those who love you, let alone trying to navigate THEIR emotions and reactions at the same time. This week's video is about how to respond to people who ask you these types of questions We go through 3 different options, and Kelly will explain the strengths and limitations of each one. Check it out, and let us know if there are others ways you have handled situations like this in the past.
#lgbt #askatherapist #identity

episodes.title: Working on Trauma without Therapy, a Therapist Explains
episodes.description:
Can you work on your trauma symptoms WITHOUT seeing a therapist? Yes and no. As a therapist, I find it impossible to ignore the harm that some therapists have done to clients during therapy work. For those reasons, and a myriad of others, it can be hard to find the fit you need with a therapist in order to deal with these types of difficulties. 

Trauma is one of those things that people have, but don't like to talk about. Or, people think they MIGHT have it, but if what has happened to them isn't as "bad" as what has happened to someone else. Add the stigma of religion into the mix, and the whole thing gets more complicated. 

Are you ready to start working on these things? Is it best for YOU to go forward without the help of a licensed therapist? I don't know. But this week's Ask a Therapist video will explain how to start to figure that out, and what to do about it if the things we address feel familiar to you. 
#religioustrauma #askatherapist #religion

episodes.title: Signs You are Gaslighting YOURSELF, and What to do About It
episodes.description:
Self Gaslighting (or Shame, as many of us know it) is pervasive in our lives. We tell ourselves to "get over" something or that our problems "are not that bad". But when we use those phrases on ourselves, we undermine one of our most basic processes...self understanding. 

We need to be kinder to ourselves. We also need to stop putting up with people who are unkind to us. And we can do neither of those things when we are beating ourselves up for having feelings. You are allowed to have whatever feeling you have, and honoring that in yourself will make you healthier!

This week's Ask a Therapist video will address what Self Gaslighting is, 5 of the most common phrases, and how they are harmful to our own development. Don't forget to comment below if you have ever encountered one of these, and how you moved past it. 
#gaslighting #shame #askatherapist

episodes.title: How do Religions Use Guilt? Ask a Therapist
episodes.description:
Guilt can be used to keep people in line or to keep them dependent. Guilt can also be used to keep people connected to one another, and to themselves (and their own boundaries). Guilt can be internal and helpful or harmful, depending on the person using it and their motives.

When someone (or some entity) with authority uses guilt to influence someone's actions, that is an inappropriate use of power. Those with authority have an obligation to those who recognize their authority, and religions notoriously have used that power in less than supportive, even damaging ways. 

This week's Ask a Therapist video will explain how guilt is used for good and used for bad, so you can spend some time processing how that has looked in your life. #askatherapist #guilt #religiousguilt

episodes.title: Therapist Explains Deconstructing My Faith
episodes.description:
People are making a lot of noise about deconstruction. It isn't a new concept, but it gains new attention every now and again. In light of the amount of religious trauma I see (and the amount of gaslighting that people experience when they speak out about that trauma), I thought I needed to clarify a few things. 

In this week's Ask a Therapist video we will explain deconstruction and outline the things that tend to happen in the lives of people when they begin to deconstruct their faith. 
#deconstruction #askatherapist #religioustrauma

episodes.title: Ask a Therapist: Toxic Femininity and Purity Culture
episodes.description:
Purity Culture tells us that there are ways that men should behave (masculine) and ways in which women should behave (feminine). Even today these values are taught in religious circles to children and youth as a way of measuring a woman's worth. 

Femininity isn't a problem, exactly. But it can become toxic when it is allowed to determine your worth or the extent to which you give yourself (and others) permission to be wholly themselves.

This week's Ask a Therapist Video is an explanation of where the idea of femininity went off the rails, and how purity culture helped make it happen. What have your experiences been with the expectation of femininity? How have you seen it be toxic?
#askatherapist #purityculture #femininity

Links to Purity Culture videos:
Lies Purity Culture Teaches Women: https://youtu.be/U3V6aJLeF1E
Modesty, Purity, and Dress code: https://youtu.be/FiRoRkXQ4sE
DisMANtaling Purity Culture: https://youtu.be/0UqEpovpoWc
Purity Culture and Virginity: https://youtu.be/by-CnGbgQ9w

episodes.title: Ask a Therapist: Gender Identity and Religious Trauma
episodes.description:
Who are you? How do you let people know who you are? and once you know who you are, and let people know who you are, what do you do then? 

Gender Identity is a huge part of who people are, and yet many who grew up in a intense religion have had to either be who they were told to be, or leave the religion altogether. But why? 

This week's Ask a Therapist video is about the intersection of Gender Identity and Religious Trauma. We also dive into the ways in which the church has infiltrated society in their expectations of gender identity and gender roles. I'd love to hear your experience with the same.
#askatherapist #genderidentity #religioustrauma

episodes.title: Ask a Therapist: How does Religious Trauma show up in Adults?
episodes.description:
"How does religious trauma show up in my adult life?"
Even for people who have recognized and are working on the impacts of religious trauma from their childhood, it can be surprising to see certain "less than healthy" routines in our lives. 

Have you noticed issues with trust? Do you have very strong responses to people manipulating you? Do you see yourself as worthy of love? These and more are indicators that an unhealthy Religious upbringing is still impacting you in your adult years. Check out this week's Ask a Therapist Video to see how it could still be impacting you, and what you can do about it. 
#religioustrauma #askatherapist #childhoodtrauma

episodes.title: One Therapist, 2 guys, & the Male Purity Culture Experience
episodes.description:
Purity Culture has proven damaging to people for generations. It isn't just perpetrated by the church, but seeps into every part of our society. 

Last week you met Kevin and Britt, two survivors of Purity Culture. Their experiences are real and they are sharing them with you! A gentle reminder, friends, if you are someone who is currently dealing with religious trauma or with high emotions regarding churches and Christians, it is ok to skip this video if you need to.  Take care of yourselves!

If you missed last week's video, part 1 of this 2 part series, feel free to check it out here:
https://youtu.be/0UqEpovpoWc

#purityculture #Askatherapist #religioustrauma

episodes.title: Ask a Therapist: DisMANtling Purity Culture; What Purity Culture Teaches Men
episodes.description:
Purity Culture has had a damaging impact on many people, and a documented impact on society as a whole. On this channel alone we've had many videos dedicated to the why's and the how's of Purity Culture on women...but time and again the question comes up: what about the impact on men?

Well I have heard you. And this week and next week we are bringing some of those answers to you. Two friends of mine have volunteered to have honest conversations about the way Purity Culture has impacted them, about what they are still working through themselves, and about what they are doing to lessen the impact on future generations. 

#PurityCulture #Religioustrauma #askatherapist

episodes.title: Ask a Therapist: Purity Culture Impacts the LGBT community
episodes.description:
Purity Culture and its negative impact on the mental health on women is something I have talked a lot about...and will continue to! But this week we are talking about Purity Culture and its impact on the LGBTQIA+ community, specific to its direct impact on Compulsory Heterosexuality (comphet). 

For many, the idea of a church based principle impacting the queer community at large does not compute. But I am here to tell you that the ways in which Purity Culture has impacted (and continues to impact) our society leaves the queer community in the margins...on purpose. Check out this video to find out why. 
#purityculture #comphet #lgbt #askatherapist

episodes.title: Ask a Therapist: Reaction to Pray Away Film on Netflix
episodes.description:
This film was incredible. And also heart breaking and frightening, with some hope and heart mixed in. As a therapist who works in the areas of LGBTQIA+ rights, deprogramming heteronormativity, and healing from religious trauma, I have had a number of people ask my opinion on this film. So, here it is!

episodes.title: Ask a Therapist: Purity Culture and Virginity
episodes.description:
Virginity was one of the ways I knew I was "worth" something, growing up in a religious community. Holding on to my "gift" was a show of love for my future husband (woops) and to God. 

One of the defining pillars of purity culture is staying "pure" until marriage. So there is a pretty strong focus on virginity...which is, of course, mostly the responsibility of the woman...or so we are taught. 

The fixation on virginity has been prevalent throughout culture, and remains a focus (to the level of preoccupation) for many today. But why? How did we get here? Is there a healthier way to understand our worth and our sexual choices? Check out this week's Ask a Therapist for answers. 

#purityculture #askatherapist #religioustrauma

episodes.title: Ask a Therapist: Religions and Emotional Manipulation
episodes.description:
Growing up I think I prayed for salvation every day. I was always afraid I didn't do it "right" because I didn't feel as ignited and SURE as I did when I first prayed about it on a retreat. It wasn't until my adult years that I began to understand that I was not the only one to feel that way, and that emotional manipulation was a cornerstone of modern religious practices. 

Religions need people to be emotionally invested so that they can keep accruing followers. But that emotional investment can quickly turn manipulative, and often does before anyone knows it. Check out this week's video to see examples of emotional manipulation by religious organizations, stick around to the end for a challenge to religious leaders and a reassurance to those who have been manipulated by religion. 
#religioustrauma #askatherapist #Emotionalmanipulation

episodes.title: Ask a Therapist: Religions and Victim Blaming
episodes.description:
For a long time I know I did it too. If someone was injured or taken advantage of, I would wonder what they did, or what their part was in the incident. Mostly it was because I wanted to know what made me different, or why I what would make sure I did not end up where they were. 

Then I grew up and realized how outrageously inappropriate it was to blame the victim, especially for my own end. 

I wish I could say that religious entities learned the same lesson. But for years, openly and behind closed doors religious organizations have created a narrative of the "at fault" victim, or the redemption of the perpetrator. So this week we are exploring how that happens, and also why. 
#religioustrauma #mentalhealth #askatherapist

episodes.title: Ask a Therapist: Modesty, purity, and dress codes. What's the deal?
episodes.description:
Recently I was unexpectedly impacted by someone's view of me...specifically when I did not in fact live up to that image of me which they had built. It was connected to the way in which we as a society treat the idea of "modesty" and "purity", as well as my opinion of what dress codes do to the psychological development of children and teens. So here is my attempt to make this make sense for people.

You see. all of the expectations we have of "proper" dress is still connected to the idea of presenting ourselves in certain ways in order to be accepted, or to be liked. It is also tied to Religion. which is why this topic is being dealt with during religious trauma month. 

Check out this video for background on modesty and dress codes, as well as an in depth explanation of the Facebook comment that sent me down this rabbit hole in the first place. 
#religioustrauma #purity #Askatherapist

episodes.title: Ask a Therapist: Power and Religion
episodes.description:
We all want power, even if it is just the power to make our own decisions. Many religions thrive on removing individual power in favor of power for the group. If you have ever been involved in an authoritative religious context, you know what I mean. 

While is may have started for "the greater good", the power of Christian Nationalism has become...intense. Behind it are fear and judgment, as well as a need to keep "us" and "them" separate. How does this impact people who are deconstructing a faith that was significant to their past? How has this lead to religious trauma? 
#Religioustrauma #AskaTherapist #deconstruction

episodes.title: Ask a Therapist: Why do queer people hate the church?
episodes.description:
This question is asked of me, REPEATEDLY. So this week I have decided to finally  answer it. In short, my answer is: You can't take a rule and apply it to all queer people. Beyond that, church trauma is real, and is disproportionately felt by those in the LGBTQIA+ community. the first half of this video answers this question...boldly and honestly. The second half holds words of peace and hopefully a message of healing for my queer friends and family. Stay tuned to the end for a bit of a personal story! 
#religioustrauma #queer #askatherapist

episodes.title: Ask a Therapist: Reaction to Things "Church People" say to those in the LGBTQIA+ Community
episodes.description:
For years, people who call themselves Christians have used certain phrases and terms against the LGBTQIA+ Community. They are used "with the best of intentions" or "to tell the truth in love", but it still comes back to the same thing; harm to those in the community. This harm is due in no small part to a lack of understanding of the reality of gender identity and sexual or affection attraction. In this week's video, I am taking the top 4 things that were submitted to me as things that have been said to them by others who qualify themselves as Christians. Check this out and see if it resonates with you!
#religioustrauma #askatherapist #LGBT

episodes.title: Ask a Therapist: Setting boundaries with Conservatives
episodes.description:
Recently there has been a lot of response to our Religious Trauma Series. Namely, people are sharing the pain that separating from their religious upbringing has caused, and confusion about how to have relationship with people from "before", now that we are who we are "now". 

Leaving a religion is never easy, especially when people you love are still involved. So what happens if you want to stay in the lives of people who are significant to you, but they are still part of that religion or religious community? How do you reconcile your healing with your desire for community and family? Check out this week's video for some of those answers. 
#religioustrauma #askatherapist #boundaries

episodes.title: Ask a Therapist: Are People Good, or Bad?
episodes.description:
"I didn't do the dishes yet, I am so lazy"
"I haven't finished this project, they are going to think I am irresponsible"
"Why can't I just motivate myself to get things done? What is wrong with me?"
Sound familiar to anyone? This is us assigning moral value to simple action. 

Recently I asked people to define a "good person". I got a LOT of really great answers;
Good people care about others, they care about the world around them, they follow the Golden Rule. Some people said good and bad don't exist, and others equated our understanding of good to things like our understanding of the bible or the way we grew up in general. All of these are well thought out, and grounded in what each person believes. 

But here's the problem. Every definition I have come across depends very much on the interpretation of the person viewing the action of others. We have become very used to assigning meaning, and therefor VALUE, to someone's actions. We also do it to our own. The Judeo-Christian culture that many of us have grown up in tells us to judge by works (out of one side of its mouth) while also saying that motivation and faith are key to judging works. WHAT?!?!

#valueassigning #AskATherapist #Goodpeople #Badpeople

episodes.title: Ask a Therapist: The Lies Purity Culture Tells Women
episodes.description:
Purity Culture, the awful hangover from the 90s that just won't won't quit. 

Like many people who grew up in a legalistically minded church setting, I had to unlearn a lot of these lies. And yet, even with all of the negative emotions, the unlearning of assumptions, and the deprogramming of so many; Purity Culture still exists and is alive and well in many churches (and some secular areas also). Check out our video, the first in our purity series, about the lies that purity culture tells women and the ways in which we can start to heal from them. 
#purityculture #askatherapist #religioustrauma

episodes.title: Ask a Therapist: Gaslighting in Churches; what it is and why it is a problem
episodes.description:
I hear people use the term Gaslighting all the time, but I'm not sure how often we look at our past and think about how Gaslighting has impacted us . First off, Gaslighting is a psychological act by a person or entity to manipulate (and gain power over) someone else. This shows up in relationships where there is an imbalance of power  As anyone who has been impacted by religious trauma knows, the power struggle in the church world is REAL, and leaders are not above using Gaslighting to keep their congregation in line. Check out this next edition of our Religious Trauma Series, and don't forget to subscribe!

episodes.title: Ask a Therapist: Toxic Positivity (and Churches)
episodes.description: This video is part of our Religious Trauma Series. Toxic Positivity is when we demand (of ourselves or others) that we "forget" the bad and "focus on the good". By negating people's pain, we comfort ourselves. This happens more in the church world than we like to admit. Watch this video to see how it happens, why it is harmful, and some ideas on how to stop doing it.

episodes.title: Ask a Therapist: What is Conversion Therapy?
episodes.description:
Conversion Therapy is still legal in 41 states. if this stat scares you as much as it scares me, check out this video. In our 2nd Pride Month video, we are dealing with a few specific questions about conversion therapy. What is it, why is it harmful, what do you do if you have been forced into it, and how to engage in conversation with those who support it.
#conversiontherapy #askatherapist #LGBTQIA

episodes.title: Ask a Therapist: I love my church, but I am gay
episodes.description:
This week we are answering a specific, and anonymous, question. 
"I love my youth pastor and I love my youth ministry, but they told me this week that I am going to Hell because I am gay. What do I do?"

This question is heart-breaking, and familiar. It BEYOND time that we stop ignoring the mental health crisis happening to queer teens, especially at the hands of the church. 

Directly from the Trevor Project:
FACTS ABOUT SUICIDE
Suicide is the 2nd leading cause of death among young people ages 10 to 24.1
LGB youth seriously contemplate suicide at almost three times the rate of heterosexual youth.2
LGB youth are almost five times as likely to have attempted suicide compared to heterosexual youth.2
Of all the suicide attempts made by youth, LGB youth suicide attempts were almost five times as likely to require medical treatment than those of heterosexual youth.2
Suicide attempts by LGB youth and questioning youth are 4 to 6 times more likely to result in injury, poisoning, or overdose that requires treatment from a doctor or nurse, compared to their straight peers.2
In a national study, 40% of transgender adults reported having made a suicide attempt. 92% of these individuals reported having attempted suicide before the age of 25.3
LGB youth who come from highly rejecting families are 8.4 times as likely to have attempted suicide as LGB peers who reported no or low levels of family rejection.4
1 out of 6 students nationwide (grades 9–12) seriously considered suicide in the past year. [5]
Each episode of LGBT victimization, such as physical or verbal harassment or abuse, increases the likelihood of self-harming behavior by 2.5 times on average.
